text: Age of peak marathon performance is 32 years in women and 34 years in men when using 1-year age groups.
Age of peak marathon performance falls in the 30-34 age group for women and 35-39 age group for men when using 5-year age groups.
Women's marathon performance at age 60-64 corresponds to approximately 90% of their peak running speed, while men reach that threshold at age 55-59.
Male marathon runners are faster than female runners by approximately 1.10 km/h on average (10.74 vs 9.64 km/h), representing a medium effect size.
The age-related decline in marathon endurance differs by sex, with women reaching peak performance earlier but potentially maintaining performance longer into older age groups.
Male marathon participants are on average 2.1 years older than female participants (43.1 vs 41.0 years).
